rationale: This supervisor structure prioritizes safe exploration by enforcing clear recovery after obstacle detection, systematic rotation to avoid oscillations, and commit states to prevent indecisive switching. Only one primary controllable action is enabled per state (except obs_front). Commit states ensure the robot completes a rotation or forward movement before reconsidering. Helper states (e.g., rotate_commit, forward_commit, escape) manage recovery and prevent immediate reversal of actions.
coverage_strategy: Prioritize moving forward when path is clear to maximize area coverage.
On obstacle detection, enter a recovery (escape) state before resuming forward movement.
Use committed rotation states to systematically scan for new paths, avoiding oscillation.
Alternate rotation direction in escape based on which side is blocked, to encourage full area exploration.
Never allow immediate return to forward after obstacle_front; always pass through escape and rotation.
Enable only one primary controllable action per state (except obs_front for safety).
